root_text.sn46.130:1.6: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ uddhambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya satta bojjhaṅgā bhāvetabbā.
root_text.sn46.130:1.8: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn46.130:1.12: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcannaṁ uddhambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya ime satta bojjhaṅgā bhāvetabbā”ti.

translation_text.sn46.130:1.4: Desire for rebirth in the realm of luminous form, desire for rebirth in the formless realm, conceit, restlessness, and ignorance.
translation_text.sn46.130:1.5: These are the five higher fetters.
translation_text.sn46.130:1.6: The seven awakening factors should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these five higher fetters.
translation_text.sn46.130:1.8: It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.130:1.9: and equanimity, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion. …”
translation_text.sn46.130:1.10: “… which have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination …”
translation_text.sn46.130:1.11: “… which slant, slope, and incline to extinguishment …
translation_text.sn46.130:1.12: The seven awakening factors should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these five higher fetters.”
